heading: Congressional reference cases
label: 1492 Congressional reference cases
text: Any bill, except a bill for a pension, may be referred by either House of Congress to the chief judge of the United States Court of Federal Claims for a report in conformity with .section 2509 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 257 (, ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 15136 Stat. 1138This section contains only the jurisdictional provision of , U.S.C., 1940 ed. The procedural provisions are incorporated in .section 257 of title 28section 2509 of this titleChanges were made in phraseology.

Amendments1992— substituted “United States Court of Federal Claims” for “United States Claims Court”.Pub. L. 102–5721982— substituted “chief judge of the United States Claims Court” for “chief commissioner of the Court of Claims”.Pub. L. 97–1641966— substituted provisions allowing any bill, except a bill for a pension, to be referred by either House of Congress to the chief commissioner of the Court of Claims for a report in conformity with for provisions giving the Court of Claims jurisdiction to report to either House of Congress on any bill referred by such House, except a bill for a pension, and to render judgment if the claim against the United States represented by the referred bill was one over which the court had jurisdiction under other Acts of Congress.Pub. L. 89–681section 2509 of this title
Effective Date of 1992 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 102–572section 911 of Pub. L. 102–572section 171 of this titleOct. 29, 1992
Effective Date of 1982 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 97–164section 402 of Pub. L. 97–164section 171 of this titleOct. 1, 1982
